12 April 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Lethal Kinetic Strikes, April 11, 2026 SOUTHCOM 12 Apr 2026 -- Applying total systemic friction on the cartels. On April 11, at the direction of the commander of U.S. Southern Command, Gen. Francis L. Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted two lethal kinetic strikes on two vessels operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • U.S. to Blockade Ships Entering or Exiting Iranian Ports CENTCOM 12 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) forces will begin implementing a blockade of all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports on April 13 at 10 a.m. ET, in accordance with the President's proclamation.
  • US Military To Blockade Iranian Ports Starting April 13 RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- The US military said it will begin a blockade of all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports as of 10 a.m. Eastern time (5:30 p.m. in Iran) on April 13 in a dramatic move following the failure of US-Iranian peace talks hours earlier.
  • Trump Says US Warships Will Blockade Strait Of Hormuz 'Immediately' RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump accused Iranian officials of violating pledges to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and said he was ordering US warships to start blockading the critical shipping strait and interdict every vessel that had paid a toll to Iran.
  • Trump Says US Naval Blockade in Strait of Hormuz Will Begin Shortly Sputnik 12 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Sunday that the United States would soon start the naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz to prevent Iran from using what he described as "extortion."
  • Trump announces naval blockade on Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump on Sunday said the US will blockade "any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz," after Saturday's talks with Iran in the Pakistani capital Islamabad failed to produce a deal to end weeks of war in the Middle East, according to Anadolu Agency.

  • No Breakthrough: US, Iran Talks Fail Following Hours-Long Session RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- Marathon talks aimed at halting the war in Iran failed without a breakthrough, as US Vice President JD Vance flew back to the United States amid major questions what comes next for the 43-day-old conflict.
  • Iran, US Failed to Reach Agreement Due to Differences on 2-3 Important Issues - Report Sputnik 12 Apr 2026 -- Iran and the United States reached an understanding on a number of issues, but disagreed on two or three important issues, which is why a deal following the talks in Islamabad failed, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei said.
  • US Delegation Returns After Iran Rejects US-Proposed Agreement - Vance Sputnik 12 Apr 2026 -- Iran decided not to accept the terms of the US-proposed agreement, US Vice President J.D. Vance, who led the US delegation at the talks in Islamabad, said.
  • 'We Have Won, Whether We Make a Deal or Not' — Trump Sputnik 12 Apr 2026 -- The president declared victory as the US-Iran talks entered third round in Islamabad.
  • JD Vance: 'We Have Not Reached Agreement' As Talks Break Off After 21 Hours RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- US Vice President JD Vance early on April 12 said that "we have not reached an agreement" and that Iran has "chosen not to accept our terms" after negotiations broke off following 21 hours of talks.
  • No Breakthrough: US, Iran Still Far Apart Following Marathon Sessions in Pakistan RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- After a grueling 21-hour marathon of high-stakes diplomacy in the Pakistani capital, negotiations between the US and Iran have fractured without a breakthrough.
  • No deal reached after hours of rare high-level US-Iran talks in decades People's Daily 12 Apr 2026 -- No agreement was reached between the United States and Iran in the latest round of talks held in Islamabad, US Vice President JD Vance said here on Sunday, as negotiations concluded without a breakthrough despite intensive engagement.
  • Iran-US talks in Islamabad end without a deal after 21 hours due to excessive US demands Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- The negotiations between Iran and the United States have come to an end without striking a deal after 21 hours of discussions in the Pakistani capital of Islamabad.

  • Fragile ceasefire in effect but maximalist US demands cast shadow over talks in Islamabad Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- As the developments on April 11 unfolded under a fragile ceasefire, Iran-US negotiations were held in Islamabad following a partial scaling back of Israeli attacks on Lebanon and Washington agreeing to release Iran's frozen assets.
  • Iran Says Talks Pause After 15 Hours, To Resume Despite 'Differences,' While US Tight-Lipped RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- Iran's government said the third session of talks between the US and Iran ended in the early hours of April 12 in Islamabad and will continue later in the day despite "differences" between the two sides.

  • Statement by Prime Minister Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 12 Apr 2026 -- "In other words, we succeeded here in crushing the nuclear program, crushing the missile program, and we also had an additional goal: To crush the regime in Iran. To weaken it to its lowest, weakest level since the establishment of the regime 47 years ago."

  • Iranian Forensics Body Identifies More Than 3,300 War Victims In The Country RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- Iranian authorities said they had identified nearly 3,400 people it said had been killed during US-Israeli strikes on the country that began on February 28.
  • US-Israeli aggression killed 258 women, 221 minors in Iran: Health official Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- The head of Iran's Emergency Organization says at least 258 women and 221 individuals under the age of 18 have been martyred in US-Israeli assaults during their war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.

  • Military ships approaching Hormuz Strait 'a violation of ceasefire', IRGC warns Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) warns that any attempt by military vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz will be deemed a violation of the two-week ceasefire with the United States.
  • IRGC: Any wrong move will turn Strait of Hormuz into 'deadly whirlpool' for enemies Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has warned the United States and its allies against any miscalculation in the Persian Gulf, as US President Donald Trump renewed his threats against the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Press TV Exclusive: US destroyers' Strait of Hormuz transit stunt failed, came close to destruction Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- The US military's attempt to sail two destroyers through the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday ended as a failed propaganda stunt timed to coincide with talks in Islamabad, a Press TV investigation has found.
  • Any Foreign Military Vessel Approaching Hormuz Under Any Pretext 'Will Be Dealt With Severely': IRGC Sputnik 12 Apr 2026 -- The Strait of Hormuz is "under reasonable control and management" and open to passage by civilian vessels "in compliance with specific regulations," the IRGC's public relations department has announced.
  • Iran's military denies US claim of vessels transit in Strait of Hormuz Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- The spokesperson for the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters has roundly dismissed the US Central Command (CENTCOM) claims about the passage of American military vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

  • Iran Negotiator Says US 'Unable' To Gain Tehran's Trust RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- The lead negotiator in Iran's talks with the United States said Washington was "unable" to win Tehran's trust during the discussions.
  • Iran 'In No Hurry' To Negotiate With US RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- Tehran has no plans for the next round of peace talks to end the war, Iran's Fars news agency reported, after Islamabad peace talks collapsed and US officials departed for Washington.
  • Iranian president conveys assessment of talks with US in phone call with Putin Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 Apr 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Sunday conveyed an assessment of US-Iran talks held in Pakistan's capital Islamabad a day prior, during a phone call with his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin, Anadolu Agency reports citing a Kremlin statement.
  • Iran says no deal with US in single meeting 'natural' amid deep mistrust Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said Sunday it was "natural" that no agreement was reached with the US in a single day of talks in Islamabad, citing a deep atmosphere of mistrust following a recent conflict, Anadolu Agency reports.
  • Iran parl. speaker warns Americans will be 'nostalgic' for cheap gasoline as U.S. threatens naval blockade Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf has issued a warning to Americans to "enjoy" today's gas prices, adding that they will soon be "nostalgic" for $4-$5 per gallon fuel as the United States threatens to impose a naval blockade on Iranian ports.
  • 'Zero lessons earned': Iran FM says US maximalism blocked progress at Islamabad talks Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- Iran's foreign minister said the United States' "maximalism, shifting goalposts, and blockade" derailed what was just inches away from becoming the "Islamabad MoU," as high-level talks between the two countries failed to produce a breakthrough after 21 hours of intensive negotiations.
  • 'We will teach you a greater lesson': Qalibaf warns Trump not to test Iran's resolve again Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf said on Sunday that the Iranian nation has proved it does not bend to US threats, warning that the country was prepared to "teach a greater lesson" if confronted again.
  • Iranian delegation leaves Pakistan after ending negotiations with US Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- A high-ranking Iranian delegation that attended an intense day of negotiations with American negotiators in Pakistan to permanently end a US-Israeli aggression against the country has left the capital, Islamabad.
  • Iran turned enemy's 'regime change' dream into 'change of governing regime in Hormuz' Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- Iranian government spokeswoman Fatemeh Mohajerani says the Islamic Republic has turned the enemy's "regime change" dream into a "change in Iran's governing regime over the Strait of Hormuz."
  • Iran will stand firm on defending rights from Hormuz to compensation: VP Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- Iran's First Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref says Tehran will stand firm on defending national rights from the Strait of Hormuz to pursuing compensation for the damage caused by the US-Israeli war against the country.
  • Iran's Parliament Speaker Expresses Gratitude to Pakistan for Facilitating Negotiations Sputnik 12 Apr 2026 -- The speaker of the Islamic Republic of Iran's Parliament, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, has expressed deep gratitude to Pakistan for its role in facilitating negotiations, calling it a friendly and brotherly country. He extended his regards to the people of Pakistan for their support.

  • Report: Tehran Aims To Restore Most Refining Capacity In 2 Months RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- Iran aims to overhaul a majority of its oil refining and distribution facilities -- which have suffered damage in US-Israeli strikes since February 28 -- within the next two months, a senior industry official was quoted as saying.
  • Iran begins rebuilding oil facilities hit by US-Israeli strikes; targets 80% capacity in 2 months Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- Iran has launched reconstruction efforts at oil infrastructure sites immediately following strikes by the US and Israel, says a senior energy official.

  • Putin Holds Phone Call With Iranian President Pezeshkian Sputnik 12 Apr 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin, in a conversation with the President of the Islamic Republic of Iran, Masoud Pezeshkian, emphasized Russia's readiness to assist in finding a political-diplomatic resolution to the conflict surrounding Iran, the Kremlin's press service reported.
  • Pezeshkian, Putin discuss ways to restore regional peace after US talks fail Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin have held a phone call hours after Pakistan-mediated negotiations between Iran and the United States ended without an agreement to permanently end the US-Israeli aggression on Iran.

  • Statement by President von der Leyen on the impact of the situation in the Middle East on the European Union European Commission 12 Apr 2026
  • Remarks by the DPM/FM at the conclusion of the Islamabad Talks Pakistan - Prime Miinister's Office 12 Apr 2026
  • Pakistan Urges Iran and US to Continue Adhering to Ceasefire - Foreign Minister Sputnik 12 Apr 2026 -- Pakistan has urged both Iran and the US to continue respecting the ceasefire, Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar said.
  • British PM Starmer Calls On US, Iran To 'Find A Way Through' RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- British Prime Minister Keir Starmer called on the United States and Iran "to find a way through" after high-level talks between the two countries failed despite marathon negotiations.
  • Saudi Arabia summons Iraqi envoy over attacks from Iraqi territory Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 Apr 2026 -- Saudi Arabia's Foreign Ministry announced on Sunday that it had summoned Iraqi envoy to Riyadh Safia Taleb Al Suhail over drone attacks targeting the kingdom and other Gulf states launched from Iraqi territory, Xinhua reports.
  • Foreign Ministry Summons Iraqi Ambassador Over Drone Attacks SPA 12 Apr 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs summoned Iraqi Ambassador to Saudi Arabia Safia Taleb Al-Suhail in response to continued blatant drone attacks and threats launched from Iraqi territory targeting Saudi Arabia and other Gulf states
  • Saudi energy facilities, East-West pipeline recover after attacks, restore operational capacity Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 Apr 2026 -- Saudi energy facilities, including the East-West pipeline that were impacted by recent attacks, have recovered and their operational capacity has been restored, Anadolu Agency reports citing the Energy Ministry.
  • Ministry of Energy: Energy Facilities and East-West Pipeline Affected by the Attacks Have Recovered and Restored Operational Capacity, Enhancing Supply Reliability SPA 12 Apr 2026 -- Further to the statement issued by the Ministry of Energy on April 9, 2026, regarding the impact on certain facilities of the Kingdom's energy sector due to the attacks, including the loss of approximately 700,000 barrels per day of pumping capacity through the East-West pipeline, and the reduction in production at the Manifa field by about 300,000 barrels per day, in addition to the impact on production at the Khurais field by about 300,000 barrels per day, the Ministry of Energy announced the success of operational and technical efforts in restoring the full pumping capacity through the East-West pipeline, amounting to approximately seven million barrels per day, and recovering the affected volumes from the Manifa field production of around 300,000 barrels per day, all within a short period of time

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Australia, Philippine, U.S. forces conduct multilateral Maritime Cooperative Activity U.S. 7th Fleet 12 Apr 2026 -- The combined forces of Australia, the Philippines and the United States conducted a multilateral Maritime Cooperative Activity (MCA) within the Philippine Exclusive Economic Zone, April 9-12, 2026. This activity demonstrated a collective commitment to strengthening regional and international cooperation in support of a free and open Indo-Pacific.

  • Kremlin Spokesman Says Too Early to Speak About NATO Collapse Sputnik 12 Apr 2026 -- Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said in an interview out on Sunday that he does not believe that a NATO collapse was likely despite differences between the United States and its European allies.

  • China's aircraft carrier Fujian to achieve full combat capability in 2026, set for far-sea drills: official media Global Times 12 Apr 2026 -- China's third aircraft carrier, the Fujian, will undergo a full transition from initial to full operational capability in 2026, with far-sea training expected, official media reported on Sunday. Chinese military affairs experts said that the Fujian has moved from construction to launch to commissioning at a fast pace, achieving operational capability in a very short time.

  • 2026 Han Kuang drills test key U.S. military rehearsals: Source CNA 12 Apr 2026 -- Taiwan's largest annual military drills are introducing several key U.S.-style rehearsal methods aimed at improving coordination and combat readiness, an unnamed senior defense official said Sunday.
  • Taiwan Navy's goodwill fleet heads to St. Lucia following St. Kitts stop CNA 12 Apr 2026 -- Taiwan's naval goodwill fleet is heading to Saint Lucia after concluding a three-day port call in Saint Kitts and Nevis on Saturday, where it was welcomed by Prime Minister Terrance Drew, according to Taiwanese embassies in the two Caribbean allies.

  • 'Days of our military sending 70 cents of every dollar to US are over': Canada PM Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says his country will reduce its longstanding dependence on the US in military spending and procurement, pledging to strengthen domestic industry and expand global partnerships.

  • INDIAN ARMY CONTINGENT DEPARTS FOR INDIA- UZBEKISTAN JOINT MILITARY EXERCISE DUSTLIK India PIB 12 Apr 2026 -- The Indian Army contingent departed today for the 7th edition of India-Uzbekistan joint military Exercise DUSTLIK. The Exercise is scheduled to be conducted at Gurumsaray Field Training Area, Namangam, Uzbekistan from 12 to 25 April 2026. Exercise DUSTLIK is a yearly event conducted alternatively in India and Uzbekistan.
  • INS TRIKAND CONCLUDES PORT CALL AT MOMBASA, KENYA India PIB 12 Apr 2026 -- INS Trikand, a frontline guided missile frigate of the Indian Navy, concluded her port call at Mombasa, Kenya, on 10 Apr 2026.

  • ASEAN unity critical in addressing maritime security challenges Philippine News Agency 12 Apr 2026 -- Unity among Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) members is crucial in addressing evolving maritime security challenges in the region.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • PM Netanyahu Visits Southern Lebanon Israel - Prime Minister 12 Apr 2026 -- Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, today, visited southern Lebanon, together with Defense Minister Israel Katz, IDF Chief-of-Staff Lt.-Gen. Eyal Zamir and Head of Northern Command Maj.-Gen. Rafi Milo.

  • Israeli forces attack Christian worshipers during Holy Saturday in occupied al-Quds Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- Israeli police forces have attacked Christian worshipers in occupied al-Quds during Holy Saturday, one of the most important Christian holidays marking the final day before Easter.

  • April 12: Hezbollah's operations against Zionist regime in response to truce breach Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- Hours after Iran and the US announced a ceasefire ending 40 days of aggression against the Islamic Republic, the Israeli regime violated the truce on Wednesday morning by launching devastating strikes on civilian areas across Lebanon.

  • Hamas calls for global action on repeated Israeli intrusions into Al-Aqsa Mosque Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas has called on the international community to assume its responsibility and take effective steps to stop repeated intrusions by Israeli settlers into the Al-Aqsa Mosque.

  • Statement on the approval by the Occupying Authorities of the establishment of 34 settlements in the Occupied West Bank League of Arab States 12 Apr 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the League of Arab States (Palestine and Occupied Arab Territories Sector) expresses its strong condemnation and profound denunciation of the Israeli occupation authorities' approval of the establishment of 34 new settlements in the West Bank, in a dangerous escalatory step that constitutes a grave and flagrant violation of international law, including the relevant Geneva Conventions, as well as resolutions of international legitimacy issued by the United Nations Security Council and General Assembly, all of which affirm the illegality of settlement activities in the occupied Palestinian territories.
  • OIC Secretary-General Condemns and Warns of the Gravity of Israel's Continued Violations of Islamic and Christian holy Sites in Occupied Al-Quds Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 12 Apr 2026 -- The Secretary-General of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC), Hissein Brahim Taha, condemned in the strongest terms the incursion of the extremist Israeli minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, into the blessed Al-Aqsa Mosque/Haram Al-Sharif, accompanied by groups of extremist settlers and under the protection of the Israeli occupation police, considering it a blatant assault on the sanctity of the blessed Al-Aqsa Mosque and a deliberate and dangerous provocation to the feelings of Muslims around the world

    • Other Conflicts

  • Statement on the Proposed Re-opening of Discussions on Oil and Gas Cooperation with China Philippines DFA 12 Apr 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) takes note of recent public statements made by certain sectors on the proposed re-opening of discussions between the Philippines and China on oil and gas cooperation.
  • Statement on Proposed Amendments to the Philippine Coast Guard-China Coast Guard Memorandum of Understanding Philippines DFA 12 Apr 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) reaffirms its 23 March 2026 Statement on the ongoing discussions to amend the Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) and the China Coast Guard (CCG).
  • DFA assures: Any joint oil exploration to follow PH laws Philippine News Agency 12 Apr 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) on Sunday assured that any potential joint oil and gas exploration with China, or any other country, will strictly adhere to the Philippine Constitution and existing laws, amid renewed discussions on energy cooperation.

  • This American Was Abducted In Kabul In 2022. His Family Is Desperate For News. RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- Mahmood Shah Habibi's parents don't get much sleep, and his daughter hasn't seen her father since she was 11 months old.

  • League Secretary-General welcomes signing by representatives of Libyan House of Representatives and High Council of State of Annex No. (1) to Unified Development Agreement League of Arab States 12 Apr 2026 -- Mr. Ahmed Aboul Gheit, Secretary-General of the League of Arab States, welcomed the signing by representatives of the Libyan House of Representatives and the High Council of State of Annex No. (1) to the Unified Development Agreement, which includes the adoption of the state's general expenditure tables for the first time in more than 13 years. He described this as an important positive development towards achieving stability and development in the country for the benefit of the Libyan people.

News Reports

  • Hungarian Opposition Leader Magyar Scores Landslide Win To Oust Orban RFE/RL 12 Apr 2026 -- Hungarian opposition leader Peter Magyar has scored a landmark election victory over Prime Minister Viktor Orban, ousting US President Donald Trump's closest ally in Europe on promises of rebuilding the country's European Union ties while curbing them with Moscow.

  • The bulk carrier is allowed to leave Sweden Swedish Coast Guard 12 Apr 2026 -- The master of the bulk carrier Hui Yuan has admitted violating the Swedish Environmental Code and has deposited money for future fines.

  • Advisory Committee Approves Maj.-Gen. Roman Gofman as the Next Director of the Mossad Israel - Prime Minister 12 Apr 2026 -- The advisory committee on senior appointments chaired by retired Supreme Court President Asher Grunis has, this evening , approved Maj.-Gen. Roman Gofman as Director of the Mossad.

  • Handala carries out unprecedented cyberattack against critical UAE Infrastructure Press TV 12 Apr 2026 -- The Handala hacking group has carried out a significant and successful cyber operation targeting the United Arab Emirates' critical infrastructure.

  • Тelephone conversation with President of Belarus Alexander Lukashenko President of Russia 12 Apr 2026

  • China successfully launches test satellite for satellite internet technology support Global Times 12 Apr 2026 -- China on Saturday launched a Smart Dragon-3 (SD-3) carrier rocket from the sea, sending a test satellite into its planned orbit to promote satellite internet technology.

  • KMT chair welcomes China's Taiwan measures as party members urge pragmatic response CNA 12 Apr 2026 -- Kuomintang (KMT) Chairperson Cheng Li-wun (鄭麗文) returned from a six-day visit to China on Sunday, welcoming Beijing's newly announced Taiwan incentive measures, as party figures and tourism representatives called on the government to respond pragmatically.
  • Taiwan criticizes overtures from Beijing; opposition reaction mixed CNA 12 Apr 2026 -- Taiwan's Cabinet dismissed the Taiwan-related policy proposals unveiled by Beijing on Sunday as a "tool of coercion," while responses from outside government ranged from caution to support.
  • China's Taiwan measures aim to deepen dependence: Scholar CNA 12 Apr 2026 -- China's newly announced incentive measures targeting Taiwan are aimed at reshaping cross-strait relations by fostering deeper economic and structural dependence, a Taiwanese scholar said Sunday.
  • China unveils 10 measures to promote Taiwan ties during KMT chair's visit CNA 12 Apr 2026 -- Chinese authorities on Sunday announced 10 policy measures targeting Taiwan, some of which appeared aimed at benefiting specific sectors such as tourism and fisheries, on the final day of Kuomintang Chairperson Cheng Li-wun's (鄭麗文) trip to China.
  • Mainland unveils package of 10 measures to boost cross-Straits exchanges, cooperation as KMT's Cheng wraps up visit Global Times 12 Apr 2026 -- Authorities on the Chinese mainland unveiled a package of 10 policies and measures to boost exchanges and cooperation across the Taiwan Straits, including pushing for resuming regular direct flights and facilitating the entry of Taiwan's agricultural and fishery products. The announcement came as Kuomintang Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun concluded a six-day visit to mainland and returned to the island of Taiwan on Sunday.
  • Mainland unveils package of policies, measures to boost ties with Taiwan Global Times 12 Apr 2026 -- Authorities on the Chinese mainland have put forward a package of 10 policies and measures to boost exchanges and cooperation across the Taiwan Strait.
  • KMT chairwoman leaves mainland after 6-day visit Global Times 12 Apr 2026 -- A delegation led by Cheng Li-wun, chairwoman of the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party, wrapped up its six-day visit to the Chinese mainland and departed Beijing for Taiwan on Sunday afternoon.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 12 Apr 2026

  • Iraqi parliament elects former environment minister Nizar Amedi as country's new president Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 Apr 2026 -- The Iraqi Parliament on Saturday elected former environment minister Nizar Amedi as the country's new president, following a decisive voting session held in the capital Baghdad, Xinhua reports.

  • PM call with the Sultan of Oman: 12 April 2026 Gov.UK 12 Apr 2026 -- The Prime Minister spoke to the Sultan of Oman, His Majesty Sultan Haitham bin Tarik al Said, this morning.
  • PM call with President Macron of France: 12 April 2026 Gov.UK 12 Apr 2026 -- The Prime Minister spoke with the President of France, Emmanuel Macron, earlier this afternoon.
